summary: Graham argues that reading can’t be replaced by faster knowledge transfer because reading also teaches writing, and writing is essential for having ideas. Writing is a form of thinking that enables discovery, especially for complex, ill-defined problems, so those who don’t read and write well are at a disadvantage.
key_points: Reading is irreplaceable because it teaches writing, not just information.
Writing is a way to develop and discover ideas, not merely record them.
Complex, ill-defined problems are best solved by writing about them.
You can’t think well without writing well, and you can’t write well without reading well.
People who want ideas must read, not just seek information.
money_quotes: Reading about x doesn't just teach you about x; it also teaches you how to write.
Writing is not just a way to convey ideas, but also a way to have them.
A good writer will almost always discover new things in the process of writing.
There is a kind of thinking that can only be done by writing.
You can't think well without writing well, and you can't write well without reading well.
key_insight: Reading well is indispensable because it trains writing, and writing is the unique form of thinking needed to develop and discover ideas.
